steps.title: Identify the signal in context
steps.content: The strongest signal is: “He avoided the clubs and usually dined alone at his hotel.” This shows Alexander had no downtime or social breaks — his time was entirely devoted to work.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined word constantly needs to mean working without breaks, since Alexander avoids clubs and dines alone — all his time goes to work. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Nonstop: without stopping or pausing — matches the sustained, uninterrupted work implied by his avoidance of all social outlets. ✔
  • Choice B — Quickly: at a fast pace — describes speed, not duration; you can work quickly for a short time, which misses the relentless continuity the passage shows. ✘
  • Choice C — Fearfully: with anxiety or dread — describes an emotional state; nothing in the passage suggests Alexander is afraid while working. ✘
  • Choice D — Enough: to a sufficient degree — says he worked sufficiently, not without interruption; it misses the unrelenting nature his isolation implies. ✘
That matches choice A — A is correct.
